target_role: Service Technician I - IT Help Desk
job_posting:
Service Technician I - IT Help Desk
Job Locations: US-Remote | US-TX-Amarillo
Job ID: 2026-3919
Category: IT

Overview:
The IT Service Technician uses specialized knowledge & skills to perform hardware support and repairs for user departments across the company. Resolves hardware, software, and communication problems/conflicts/defects.

Position: Remote-based within Continental US. Shift: 10am-7pm CST.

Responsibilities:
- Investigate, analyze, and resolve hardware and associated software and communications problems with computer systems
- Perform complex hardware repairs and maintenance on PC-based computers and peripherals
- Provide advanced hardware technical assistance and support
- Troubleshoot and resolve system hardware, software, and communications failures and conflicts
- Install, integrate, and configure new computer systems and hardware for users
- Provision, modify, and deprovision user access for systems and applications per access policies and SOPs
- Review, prioritize, and process problem reports; document project progress
- Document technical processes and support procedures; contribute to standardization
- Assist in audits: quarterly system access, software, licensing
- Maintain high-level customer service standards
- Follow procedures for call length and escalation process
- Resolve tickets to meet set SLAs

Qualifications:
- High School or equivalent required; Bachelor's in MIS/CS or equivalent preferred
- 1-2 years PC maintenance/repair, application software support
- Microsoft Office required; hardware/software certifications a plus
- ITIL or Cobit knowledge; retail pharmacy ops desirable; corporate environment desirable
- Advanced repairs/upgrades, troubleshooting, install/configure systems, customer service, self-motivated, mentor co-workers

score_scope.chatgpt: Presentation-only: format, polish, grouping, bullet quality vs 9.4 anchor

rationale.chatgpt: Strong resume. Fixed placeholder Education issue, removed em dash from summary, kept one-page polished structure. Clearly better than prior drafts and close to 9.4 anchor. Summary is clean and human: role identity, support scope, workflow in plain language. BEAT THE BOMB shows end-to-end help desk handling, Windows/hardware/network, TCP/IP/DNS, mobile support, hardware replacement, RustDesk, SOPs/runbooks, 75% wait-time metric. Education now correct (BA Political Science, Rowan, 3.80 Cum Laude). Grouped skills polished and easy to skim, not a keyword dump.

key_strengths: Summary gives role identity, support scope, and workflow in plain language without awkward 'candidate' phrasing
BEAT THE BOMB covers end-to-end help desk, Windows/hardware/network, TCP/IP/DNS, RustDesk, SOPs/runbooks, 75% metric
Education section present and correct: BA Political Science, Rowan University, GPA 3.80 Cum Laude
Grouped skills section is polished and scannable, not a keyword dump
key_weaknesses: Date ranges use plain hyphens (Sept 2024 - Mar 2026) — less polished than 'Sept 2024 to Mar 2026' or en dash
Project section has two bullets; second is a sentence fragment ('Reflects the same support habit...')
Skills section slightly long vs 9.4 anchor elegance
specific_fixes: Use 'Sept 2024 to Mar 2026' instead of hyphen date ranges throughout
Compress project into one bullet merging pipeline description and support-habit reflection
Optional: trim skills section length to match 9.4 anchor density
tier_definition_match: Matches target presentation bar at 9.1. Iteration after 4_target_9.0 (added Education, dropped 'candidate', cleaner summary). With date cleanup and one-bullet project, estimated 9.25-9.3. Pairs with 2_mid_7.6 and 4_target_9.0 as polish progression for same VytlOne role.
